task_01.name: sim
task_01.title: Sparse Hessian Deconvolution
task_01.description:
This code implements a **Sparse Hessian Deconvolution** algorithm for microscopy image reconstruction. The pipeline consists of:

1. **Data Preprocessing**: Load image, estimate and subtract background using wavelet decomposition, normalize, optionally upsample
2. **Sparse Hessian Reconstruction**: ADMM-based optimization minimizing Hessian regularization + L1 sparsity + data fidelity
3. **Iterative Deconvolution**: Post-processing with Richardson-Lucy or Landweber deconvolution
4. **Evaluation**: Compute PSNR, SSIM, MSE against expected output

---

task_02.name: ptyrad
task_02.title: Ptychography Phase Retrieval
task_02.description:
This code implements **ptychographic reconstruction using automatic differentiation (AD)**. Ptychography is a computational imaging technique that reconstructs a complex-valued object (amplitude and phase) from a series of diffraction patterns collected at overlapping scan positions.

**Pipeline Stages:**
1. `data_preprocessing`: Load parameters, initialize variables, loss functions, and constraints
2. `forward_model`: Multislice ptychographic forward operator computing predicted diffraction patterns
3. `inversion`: Gradient-based optimization to minimize discrepancy between predicted and measured patterns
4. `evaluation`: Compute reconstruction quality metrics and statistics

**Physical Model:** The forward operator implements `y = A(x)` where:
- `x`: Latent variables (object amplitude/phase, probe, tilts, thickness, position shifts)
- `A`: Multislice ptychographic propagation model
- `y`: Predicted diffraction pattern intensities

---

task_03.name: lfm
task_03.title: Light Field Microscopy
task_03.description:
This code implements a Light Field Microscopy (LFM) 3D volume reconstruction pipeline using Richardson-Lucy iterative deconvolution. The pipeline consists of five stages:

1. **Data Loading**: Load calibration image, configuration, and raw lenslet image
2. **Geometry Computation**: Calculate camera parameters, lenslet geometry, and forward/backward projection operators
3. **Image Correction**: Apply geometric transformation and normalization to raw lenslet image
4. **Volume Reconstruction**: Iterative Richardson-Lucy deconvolution with optional anti-aliasing filtering
5. **Visualization**: Generate error convergence plot and input/output comparison

The algorithm reconstructs a 3D volume from a 2D light field image by iteratively refining an initial estimate using forward and backward projections through the light field imaging model.

---

task_04.name: PyTomography-main
task_04.title: PET/SPECT Reconstruction
task_04.description:
This code implements a **SPECT (Single Photon Emission Computed Tomography) image reconstruction pipeline** using the **Ordered Subsets Expectation Maximization (OSEM)** algorithm. The pipeline consists of:

1. **Phantom Generation**: Create a 3D Shepp-Logan phantom as ground truth
2. **Forward Projection**: Simulate SPECT acquisition by projecting the 3D object onto 2D detector planes at multiple angles
3. **Noise Simulation**: Add Poisson noise to projections to simulate realistic photon counting statistics
4. **Iterative Reconstruction**: Recover the 3D object from noisy projections using OSEM
5. **Evaluation**: Compute image quality metrics (PSNR, SSIM)

The system matrix implements rotation-based projection/backprojection with padding to handle rotation artifacts.

---

task_05.name: s2ISM-main
task_05.title: Image Scanning Microscopy
task_05.description:
This pipeline implements a simulation and reconstruction framework for Image Scanning Microscopy (ISM) data. The algorithm performs:

1. **Phantom Generation**: Create synthetic biological sample (tubulin filaments) at multiple z-planes
2. **PSF Simulation**: Calculate Point Spread Functions for SPAD detector array with optimal out-of-focus plane determination
3. **Forward Model**: Generate synthetic ISM measurements via convolution and Poisson noise
4. **Inverse Problem**: Reconstruct the original object using Richardson-Lucy maximum likelihood deconvolution
5. **Evaluation**: Assess reconstruction quality using image quality metrics

The core reconstruction uses an iterative expectation-maximization approach with FFT-accelerated convolutions for multi-plane, multi-detector ISM data.

task_06.name: semiblindpsfdeconv-master
task_06.title: Semi-Blind PSF Deconvolution
task_06.description:
This code implements a **semi-blind spatially-variant deconvolution** pipeline for image restoration. The system consists of:

1. **Forward Model**: Simulates spatially-variant Gaussian blur using a grid of PSFs with bilinear interpolation weights, plus additive Gaussian noise
2. **PSF Estimation**: Uses a pre-trained ResNet CNN with sliding window inference to estimate local blur parameters (FWHM_x, FWHM_y) across the image
3. **Inverse Model**: Performs spatially-variant Richardson-Lucy deconvolution with Total Variation (TV) regularization
4. **Evaluation**: Computes PSNR and SSIM between ground truth and restored images

task_07.name: oopao_sh_wfs
task_07.title: Shack-Hartmann Wavefront Sensing
task_07.description:
This code implements a closed-loop Adaptive Optics (AO) simulation using a Shack-Hartmann Wavefront Sensor (SH-WFS). The pipeline consists of:

1. **System Initialization**: Configure telescope, source, atmosphere, deformable mirror (DM), and wavefront sensor
2. **Calibration**: Compute reference PSF, reference slopes, interaction matrix via push-pull, and reconstructor via SVD
3. **Closed-Loop Control**: Run integral control to minimize residual wavefront error
4. **Evaluation**: Compute Strehl ratio history and performance statistics

The inversion problem finds DM commands that minimize wavefront aberrations introduced by atmospheric turbulence.

---

task_08.name: oopao_image_zernike
task_08.title: Zernike Wavefront Estimation
task_08.description:
This code implements an adaptive optics simulation pipeline with two main components:

1. **Forward Model**: Computes the Point Spread Function (PSF) from a phase aberration map using Fourier optics principles
2. **Inverse Model**: Decomposes atmospheric turbulence (OPD maps) into Zernike polynomial coefficients using least-squares fitting

The pipeline stages are:
1. System initialization (telescope, source, atmosphere, Zernike basis)
2. Explicit Zernike mode generation using radial polynomial formulas
3. Forward PSF computation via FFT
4. Iterative atmospheric OPD decomposition
5. Reconstruction quality evaluation

task_09.name: bayhunt
task_09.title: Bayesian Seismic Inversion
task_09.description:
This code implements a **Bayesian MCMC inversion** for joint inversion of seismic data: **surface wave dispersion (SWD)** and **receiver function (RF)** data. The goal is to estimate subsurface shear velocity (Vs) structure as a function of depth using a transdimensional approach where the number of layers is also inferred.

**Pipeline stages:**
1. `data_preprocessing`: Generate synthetic test data, add noise, load configuration
2. `forward_modeling`: Compute predicted SWD and RF from velocity model
3. `mcmc_inversion`: Run parallel MCMC chains to sample posterior distribution
4. `evaluation`: Save final model distribution and generate diagnostic plots

---

task_10.name: pyeit
task_10.title: Electrical Impedance Tomography
task_10.description:
This code implements 3D Electrical Impedance Tomography (EIT) reconstruction using the Jacobian (JAC) method. The pipeline consists of:

1. **Mesh Generation**: Create 3D tetrahedral mesh with electrodes on a ball-shaped domain
2. **Protocol Setup**: Define excitation and measurement patterns for adjacent-mode EIT
3. **Forward Problem**: Solve Poisson equation via FEM to compute boundary voltages from conductivity
4. **Inverse Problem**: Linearized reconstruction using Jacobian-based Tikhonov regularization
5. **Evaluation**: Compute reconstruction statistics and visualize results

The algorithm reconstructs element-wise conductivity changes from differential boundary voltage measurements.

metrics.eval_type: Conductivity distribution error (EIT)

task_11.name: bpm
task_11.title: Beam Propagation ODT
task_11.description:
This code implements a Beam Propagation Method (BPM) inverse solver for Optical Diffraction Tomography (ODT). The pipeline reconstructs the 3D refractive index perturbation (delta_ri) of a sample from measured input and output complex optical fields under multiple illumination angles.

**Pipeline Stages:**
1. **Data Preprocessing**: Load fields, compute propagation kernels, propagate fields to inlet/outlet planes
2. **Initialization**: Zero-initialize refractive index volume and FISTA momentum variables
3. **Iterative Optimization**: FISTA-accelerated gradient descent with adjoint-based gradient computation
4. **Regularization**: Total Variation (TV) denoising with value range clamping and optional sparsity
5. **Evaluation**: Compute ROI statistics and loss metrics

metrics.eval_type: Refractive index reconstruction loss (BPM)

task_12.name: diff
task_12.title: Differentiable Optical Metrology
task_12.description:
This code implements **optical lens metrology via differentiable ray tracing**. The pipeline recovers physical lens parameters (surface curvatures, thickness, position, tilt angles) from measured ray-display intersection points using gradient-based optimization.

**Pipeline stages:**
1. `data_preprocessing`: Load calibration data, measurement images, solve for intersection points
2. `forward_operator`: Ray tracing through lens system to compute predicted intersection points
3. `inversion`: Optimize lens parameters to minimize discrepancy between measured and predicted intersections
4. `evaluation`: Compute displacement error and generate visualizations

metrics.eval_type: Optical system optimization metrics (differentiable optics)

task_13.name: insar
task_13.title: InSAR Phase Unwrapping
task_13.description:
This code implements an InSAR (Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar) phase unwrapping algorithm using ADMM (Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers). The pipeline recovers continuous unwrapped phase values from wrapped phase measurements (constrained to [-π, π]) by solving a sparse optimization problem that minimizes the p-norm of gradient residuals.

**Pipeline Stages:**
1. `data_preprocessing`: Load interferogram, extract wrapped phase, compute differentiation matrices and wrapped gradients
2. `forward_operator`: Compute spatial gradients of unwrapped phase estimate
3. `run_inversion`: ADMM optimization loop to solve the unwrapping problem
4. `evaluation`: Compute statistics and save results

metrics.eval_type: Phase unwrapping residual (InSAR)

task_14.name: pat
task_14.title: Photoacoustic Tomography
task_14.description:
This code implements a **Photoacoustic Tomography (PAT) reconstruction pipeline** with oxygen saturation estimation. The pipeline consists of five stages:

1. **Data Loading & Preprocessing**: Load HDF5 data, apply bandpass filtering, extract envelope via Hilbert transform
2. **Image Reconstruction**: Delay-and-Sum (DAS) backprojection algorithm
3. **Spectral Unmixing**: Linear unmixing to estimate hemoglobin concentrations
4. **sO2 Calculation**: Compute oxygen saturation from hemoglobin concentrations
5. **Evaluation**: Compute metrics and generate visualizations

The forward model relates initial pressure distribution to detected time-domain signals based on acoustic wave propagation.

metrics.eval_type: SO2 estimation accuracy (photoacoustic)

task_15.name: lensless_admm
task_15.title: Lensless ADMM Reconstruction
task_15.description:
This code implements an ADMM (Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers) algorithm for lensless image reconstruction. The pipeline reconstructs a latent image from a raw sensor measurement using a known Point Spread Function (PSF). The algorithm solves a regularized inverse problem with Total Variation (TV) regularization and non-negativity constraints.

**Pipeline Stages:**
1. `data_preprocessing`: Load and normalize PSF and measurement data
2. `initialization`: Set up padded arrays, precompute FFT matrices
3. `main_process`: ADMM iterative optimization
4. `evaluation`: Compute statistics and save results

metrics.eval_type: Visual reconstruction quality (lensless ADMM)

task_16.name: lensless_dl
task_16.title: Lensless Deep Learning
task_16.description:
This code implements an image deconvolution algorithm to recover a latent image from blurred measurements using a known Point Spread Function (PSF). The algorithm uses Accelerated Proximal Gradient Descent (APGD), also known as FISTA (Fast Iterative Shrinkage-Thresholding Algorithm), with FFT-based convolution for computational efficiency.

**Pipeline stages:**
1. `data_preprocessing`: Load and prepare PSF and measurement images
2. `forward_operator`: FFT-based convolution setup
3. `run_inversion`: APGD optimization loop
4. `evaluate_results`: Save and report reconstruction statistics

metrics.eval_type: Visual reconstruction quality (lensless DL)

task_17.name: pnp_cassi
task_17.title: CASSI Hyperspectral Imaging
task_17.description:
This code implements **Generalized Alternating Projection with Total Variation denoising (GAP-TV)** for reconstructing 3D spectral image cubes from 2D compressed measurements in Coded Aperture Snapshot Spectral Imaging (CASSI) systems.

**Pipeline Stages:**
1. Data loading and preprocessing (mask preparation, measurement simulation)
2. Iterative GAP-TV reconstruction
3. Evaluation and result output

**Physical Model:** CASSI uses a coded aperture mask and dispersive element to encode multiple spectral channels into a single 2D measurement through spatial shifting and summation.

task_18.name: fpm_inr
task_18.title: Fourier Ptychographic Microscopy
task_18.description:
This code implements **FPM-INR (Fourier Ptychographic Microscopy with Implicit Neural Representations)** for high-resolution complex image reconstruction. The pipeline:

1. **Data Preprocessing**: Load low-resolution intensity measurements from multiple LED illuminations, compute frequency-space parameters, pupil support, and angular spectrum propagation kernel
2. **Neural Representation**: Use learnable 3D feature tensors with bilinear interpolation and MLP rendering to represent amplitude and phase images
3. **Forward Model**: Simulate FPM image formation via FFT, sub-aperture extraction, pupil masking, and defocus propagation
4. **Optimization**: Minimize intensity mismatch using Adam optimizer with Smooth L1 loss
5. **Evaluation**: Compute reconstruction statistics and save results

task_19.name: flfm
task_19.title: Fourier Light Field Microscopy
task_19.description:
This code implements a **Richardson-Lucy (RL) deconvolution** algorithm for reconstructing a 3D volume from a 2D widefield microscopy measurement. The forward model simulates depth-varying blur by convolving each z-slice of a 3D object with a corresponding 2D PSF and summing the results. The inverse problem recovers the 3D object using multiplicative iterative updates derived from maximum likelihood estimation under Poisson noise assumptions.

**Pipeline Stages:**
1. `data_preprocessing`: Generate synthetic PSF, ground truth object, and simulated measurement
2. `initialization`: Set initial 3D estimate
3. `main_process`: Execute Richardson-Lucy iterative updates
4. `evaluation`: Compute reconstruction quality metrics

task_20.name: lenstronomy_simple_ring
task_20.title: Gravitational Lensing (Ring)
task_20.description:
This code implements a gravitational lens modeling inverse problem using the lenstronomy library. The pipeline:
1. Simulates a lensed galaxy image with realistic noise (Poisson + Gaussian)
2. Fits lens mass model, source light, and lens light parameters using optimization (PSO) followed by MCMC sampling
3. Evaluates fit quality through chi-squared statistics and parameter recovery

The forward model computes a lensed image given: lens mass distribution (SIE + external shear), background source light (Sersic profile), and foreground lens light (Sersic profile), convolved with a PSF.

metrics.eval_type: Lens model parameter error (gravitational lensing)

task_21.name: lenstronomy_quad_quasar
task_21.title: Gravitational Lensing (Quad)
task_21.description:
This code implements a gravitational lensing simulation and parameter inference pipeline using the `lenstronomy` library. The pipeline consists of:
1. **Data Generation**: Simulate a strong gravitational lensing system with lens galaxy, lensed source, and point sources
2. **Noise Addition**: Add realistic Poisson (shot) noise and Gaussian background noise
3. **Forward Modeling**: Compute predicted images given model parameters
4. **Parameter Inference**: Fit lens model parameters using Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) followed by Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC)
5. **Evaluation**: Compare fitted parameters against ground truth and compute residuals

metrics.eval_type: Lens model parameter error (gravitational lensing)

task_22.name: lenstronomy_double_quasar
task_22.title: Gravitational Lensing (Double)
task_22.description:
This pipeline performs gravitational lens modeling—an inverse problem that recovers lens mass distribution and source properties from observed lensed images. The workflow consists of three stages:

1. **Data Generation**: Simulate mock strong lensing observations with realistic noise (Poisson + Gaussian)
2. **Parameter Inversion**: Fit lens model parameters using Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) followed by Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) sampling
3. **Evaluation**: Compare fitted parameters to ground truth and compute image residual statistics

The forward model computes a lensed image given lens mass parameters, source light distribution, lens galaxy light, and point source positions, convolved with a PSF.

metrics.eval_type: Lens model parameter error (gravitational lensing)

task_23.name: lenstronomy_shapelets
task_23.title: Gravitational Lensing (Shapelets)
task_23.description:
This code implements a gravitational lensing inverse problem for source reconstruction. The pipeline:
1. **Data Simulation**: Generate a synthetic lensed image of a complex source galaxy with realistic noise
2. **Linear Inversion**: Reconstruct the source using shapelet basis functions via weighted least squares
3. **Forward Operator**: Map shapelet coefficients through lensing and PSF convolution to image space
4. **Evaluation**: Compute reconstruction quality metrics

The core algorithm solves a linear inverse problem where the unknown source is parameterized by shapelet basis function coefficients.

task_24.name: lenstronomy_host_decomp
task_24.title: Gravitational Lensing (Host)
task_24.description:
This pipeline performs astronomical image simulation and model fitting for a quasar-host galaxy system. The workflow consists of:

1. **Data Generation**: Simulate an astronomical image containing a point source (quasar) and extended host galaxy (disk + bulge components) with realistic noise
2. **Forward Modeling**: Compute predicted images given model parameters using PSF convolution
3. **Parameter Inversion**: Recover model parameters from noisy observations using Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO)
4. **Evaluation**: Assess reconstruction quality through residual analysis

The system uses lenstronomy library for astronomical image modeling with Sérsic light profiles and Gaussian PSF convolution.

metrics.eval_type: Host galaxy decomposition error (gravitational lensing)

task_25.name: dpi_task1
task_25.title: DPI Radio Interferometry
task_25.description:
This code implements Deep Probabilistic Imaging (DPI) for radio interferometry image reconstruction. The pipeline:
1. Loads UV-FITS observation data and constructs forward operators (DFT matrix, closure phase mappings)
2. Trains a RealNVP normalizing flow to generate posterior image samples
3. Optimizes using closure phase data fidelity, entropy regularization, flux constraints, and log-determinant terms
4. Evaluates reconstruction quality via flux error and closure phase residuals

The approach enables uncertainty quantification by sampling from the learned posterior distribution.

metrics.eval_type: Interferometry reconstruction quality (DPI)

task_26.name: dpi_task2
task_26.title: DPI MRI Reconstruction
task_26.description:
This algorithm performs MRI image reconstruction from undersampled k-space measurements using Deep Probabilistic Imaging (DPI). The approach uses a RealNVP normalizing flow as a generative prior, optimizing the flow parameters to produce images consistent with observed k-space data while incorporating regularization.

**Pipeline Stages:**
1. `data_preprocessing`: Load MRI image and mask, compute noisy k-space measurements
2. `initialization`: Set up RealNVP model, scale factor, and optimizer
3. `main_process`: Iterative optimization via stochastic gradient descent
4. `evaluation`: Compute reconstruction quality metrics (RMSE, PSNR)

task_27.name: dpi_task3
task_27.title: DPI CT Reconstruction
task_27.description:
This code implements a variational inference approach for radio interferometric image reconstruction using normalizing flows. The pipeline:

1. **Data Preprocessing**: Load UVFITS observation data, compute DFT matrices, and build closure quantity index maps
2. **Generative Model**: A RealNVP normalizing flow transforms standard Gaussian samples to geometric image parameters
3. **Forward Model**: Geometric parameters → Image → Visibilities → Closure phases/amplitudes
4. **Optimization**: Minimize alpha-divergence between approximate posterior and true posterior via stochastic gradient descent
5. **Evaluation**: Generate posterior samples and compute reconstruction statistics

The geometric model produces crescent-shaped images (motivated by black hole imaging) with optional Gaussian nuisance components.

metrics.eval_type: CT reconstruction quality (DPI)

task_28.name: AMICO-master
task_28.title: NODDI Microstructure
task_28.description:
This pipeline implements NODDI (Neurite Orientation Dispersion and Density Imaging) parameter estimation using the AMICO (Accelerated Microstructure Imaging via Convex Optimization) framework. The algorithm:

1. **Data Loading**: Loads diffusion-weighted MRI data, acquisition scheme, and brain mask
2. **DTI Preprocessing**: Fits diffusion tensor to obtain principal fiber directions
3. **Forward Model Generation**: Creates a dictionary of NODDI signal kernels across parameter space
4. **Sparse Inversion**: Solves a regularized linear inverse problem per voxel using LASSO
5. **Parameter Extraction**: Computes NDI, ODI, and FWF from fitted coefficients

The NODDI model comprises three tissue compartments: intra-cellular (Watson-distributed sticks), extra-cellular (hindered diffusion), and isotropic (free water/CSF).

task_29.name: carspy-main
task_29.title: CARS Spectroscopy Fitting
task_29.description:
This task recovers gas temperature from N2 Coherent Anti-Stokes Raman Spectroscopy (CARS) spectra.

Pipeline:
1. **Forward model**: Generate theoretical CARS spectra from molecular physics, including rovibrational populations, susceptibility terms, and collisional narrowing.
2. **Synthetic measurement**: Create noisy observed spectra from a known ground-truth temperature.
3. **Inversion**: Use nonlinear least-squares fitting to estimate temperature by minimizing residuals between measured and modeled spectra.
4. **Evaluation**: Compare estimated temperature and reconstructed spectra against ground truth using error metrics.

task_30.name: caustics-main
task_30.title: Gravitational Lensing (Caustics)
task_30.description:
This code solves a **gravitational lensing source reconstruction** inverse problem. Given an observed lensed image, the algorithm recovers the original unlensed source image by optimizing through a differentiable forward model.

**Pipeline stages:**
1. `data_preprocessing`: Load/create source image, generate synthetic lensed observation
2. `forward_model`: Simulate gravitational lensing using SIE lens + ray tracing
3. `inversion`: Gradient-based optimization to recover source from observation
4. `evaluation`: Compute reconstruction quality metrics (PSNR, SSIM)

task_31.name: CT-and-MR-Perfusion-Tool-main
task_31.title: CT/MR Perfusion Imaging
task_31.description:
This code implements a **CT/MR Perfusion (CTP/MRP) Analysis Pipeline** that solves an inverse problem to extract hemodynamic perfusion parameters from 4D medical imaging data. The pipeline consists of four stages:

1. **Data Preprocessing**: Load NIfTI images, reorient, downsample, smooth, and generate brain masks
2. **Forward Operator**: Convert raw signal intensity to concentration-time curves (CTC)
3. **Inversion (SVD Deconvolution)**: Estimate Arterial Input Function (AIF) and solve the deconvolution problem to extract perfusion maps
4. **Evaluation**: Compare generated maps against reference using image quality metrics

The core inverse problem models tissue concentration as a convolution: `C(t) = CBF · (AIF ⊗ R)(t)`, solved via truncated SVD regularization.

task_32.name: DiffuserCam-Tutorial-master
task_32.title: Diffuser Camera Reconstruction
task_32.description:
This code implements a **lensless camera image reconstruction** algorithm using **ADMM (Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers)**. The pipeline reconstructs a scene from a coded sensor measurement captured through a diffuser (characterized by a Point Spread Function).

**Pipeline Stages:**
1. **Data Preprocessing**: Load and downsample PSF and sensor data, apply background subtraction and normalization
2. **Precomputation**: Compute frequency-domain operators and division matrices for efficient ADMM updates
3. **ADMM Iteration**: Alternating updates of primal, auxiliary, and dual variables
4. **Post-processing**: Crop reconstructed image to sensor size, enforce non-negativity
5. **Evaluation**: Compute PSNR and SSIM on synthetic data with known ground truth

task_33.name: dmipy-master
task_33.title: Diffusion MRI Microstructure
task_33.description:
This code implements a **Ball & Stick model** fitting pipeline for diffusion MRI (dMRI) data. The pipeline:
1. Generates synthetic multi-shell dMRI acquisition data with Rician noise
2. Defines a forward model combining isotropic (Ball) and anisotropic (Stick) diffusion compartments
3. Performs Non-Linear Least Squares (NLLS) inversion using L-BFGS-B optimization
4. Evaluates parameter recovery accuracy and signal reconstruction quality

The Ball & Stick model represents tissue microstructure as a mixture of free water diffusion (Ball) and restricted diffusion along a fiber orientation (Stick).

task_34.name: hcipy-master
task_34.title: High Contrast Imaging
task_34.description:
This code implements a **closed-loop adaptive optics (AO) simulation** for astronomical imaging. The pipeline consists of:

1. **System Setup**: Define telescope parameters, pupil grid, aperture, wavefront sensor (WFS), and deformable mirror (DM)
2. **Calibration**: Build interaction matrix via push-pull probing of DM modes, then invert using Tikhonov regularization
3. **Closed-Loop Control**: Iteratively sense wavefront errors, estimate slopes, reconstruct commands, and apply corrections
4. **Evaluation**: Compute image quality metrics (PSNR, Strehl ratio) for a binary star science scene

The simulation models a Shack-Hartmann wavefront sensor with centroid-based slope estimation and disk harmonic DM influence functions.

task_35.name: mne-master
task_35.title: MEG/EEG Source Localization
task_35.description:
This code implements **Minimum Norm Estimate (MNE) source localization** with **dSPM (dynamic Statistical Parametric Mapping)** normalization for MEG/EEG inverse problems. The pipeline:

1. **Data Preprocessing**: Load MEG sensor data, compute noise covariance from baseline, extract forward model (gain matrix), compute SSP projection matrix
2. **Whitening**: Transform the problem to whitened space using noise covariance eigendecomposition
3. **Inverse Operator Construction**: Use SVD-based regularized least squares to compute the inverse operator
4. **dSPM Normalization**: Normalize source estimates by noise variance for statistical interpretation
5. **Evaluation**: Compare against MNE-Python reference implementation

task_36.name: MPIRF-master
task_36.title: Magnetic Particle Imaging
task_36.description:
This code implements a **Magnetic Particle Imaging (MPI) reconstruction pipeline** consisting of four stages:
1. **Data Preprocessing**: Initialize physical constants, scanner parameters, spatial/temporal grids, drive fields, and generate a P-shaped phantom
2. **Forward Operator**: Compute the system matrix using Langevin function derivatives and simulate frequency-domain measurements
3. **Inversion**: Reconstruct the particle concentration image using regularized Kaczmarz algorithm
4. **Evaluation**: Compute PSNR and SSIM metrics comparing reconstruction to ground truth

The pipeline solves the inverse problem: given induced voltage measurements, recover the spatial distribution of magnetic nanoparticle concentration.

task_37.name: MRE-elast-master
task_37.title: MR Elastography
task_37.description:
This code implements **Magnetic Resonance Elastography (MRE) reconstruction** - an inverse problem that recovers tissue stiffness (Young's modulus E) from measured displacement fields. The pipeline consists of:

1. **Data Preprocessing**: Load image, generate triangular mesh, compute FEM matrices with boundary conditions
2. **Forward Model**: Solve elasticity equation K(E)u = f to generate synthetic displacement data
3. **Noise Addition**: Add heteroscedastic Gaussian noise to displacement measurements
4. **Initialization**: Generate initial stiffness estimate from noisy ground truth with thresholding
5. **Inversion**: Proximal optimization (Generalized Forward-Backward) with Mahalanobis data fidelity, TV regularization, and box constraints
6. **Evaluation**: Compute CNR and RMSE metrics

metrics.eval_type: Elasticity modulus reconstruction CNR (MRE)

task_38.name: mrf-reconstruction-mlmir2020-master
task_38.title: MR Fingerprinting
task_38.description:
This code implements an **Invertible Neural Network (INN)** for **Magnetic Resonance Fingerprinting (MRF)** parameter estimation. The pipeline:

1. **Data Loading**: Load MRF fingerprints and corresponding tissue parameters from disk
2. **Model Construction**: Build an INN using Real-valued Non-Volume Preserving (RNVP) coupling blocks
3. **Training**: Optimize bidirectional mapping loss (forward + backward MSE)
4. **Inference**: Perform parameter estimation via inverse pass through trained INN
5. **Evaluation**: Compare reconstructed parameters and predicted fingerprints against ground truth

The INN learns bijective mappings: **x (parameters) ↔ y (fingerprints)**, enabling both forward simulation and inverse parameter estimation.

metrics.eval_type: MR fingerprinting matching accuracy

task_39.name: mripy-master
task_39.title: MRI ADMM-TV Reconstruction
task_39.description:
This code implements **Compressed Sensing MRI Reconstruction** using ADMM (Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers) with Total Variation (TV) regularization for parallel imaging. The pipeline consists of:

1. **Data Preprocessing**: Load multi-coil k-space data, estimate coil sensitivity maps via ESPIRiT, generate undersampling mask
2. **Forward Model**: Sensitivity encoding → 2D FFT → Undersampling mask application
3. **Inverse Problem**: ADMM optimization minimizing data fidelity + TV regularization
4. **Evaluation**: Compute PSNR against fully-sampled reference

The algorithm reconstructs a 2D MRI image from undersampled multi-coil k-space measurements.

task_40.name: oct-cbort-main
task_40.title: OCT Structural Imaging
task_40.description:
This code implements a **Spectral-Domain OCT Reconstruction Pipeline** that transforms raw interference fringe data into a structural intensity image. The pipeline consists of four stages:

1. **Data Preprocessing**: Load metadata, calibration files, and raw OCT data; precompute reconstruction maps
2. **Forward Operator**: Apply spectral processing chain (FFT → Zoom → Dechirp → Dispersion Compensation → FFT) to reconstruct complex tomograms
3. **Inversion**: Convert complex tomograms to log-compressed intensity image with contrast scaling
4. **Evaluation**: Compute image statistics and save visualization

The algorithm is a **deterministic signal processing pipeline** (non-iterative) that performs inverse scattering from k-space (wavenumber) to z-space (depth).

metrics.eval_type: OCT structural image quality

task_41.name: phasorpy-main
task_41.title: FLIM Phasor Analysis
task_41.description:
This code implements a **Phasor Transform** pipeline for Fluorescence Lifetime Imaging Microscopy (FLIM) data analysis. The pipeline consists of:

1. **Data Loading**: Load time-domain FLIM image stack from TIFF file
2. **Forward Operator**: Transform time-domain signal to phasor (frequency) domain using FFT
3. **Filtering**: Apply median filtering and intensity thresholding on phasor coordinates
4. **Inverse Operator**: Reconstruct time-domain signal from filtered phasor coordinates
5. **Evaluation**: Compute reconstruction quality metrics

The phasor approach represents periodic signals as points in a 2D coordinate system (G, S) derived from Fourier coefficients.

task_42.name: PtyLab-main
task_42.title: Ptychographic Reconstruction
task_42.description:
This code implements a **Ptychographic Iterative Engine (ePIE)** reconstruction algorithm for coherent diffraction imaging. The pipeline:

1. **Data Loading**: Load ptychogram (diffraction patterns), scan positions, and geometry parameters from HDF5
2. **Preprocessing**: Calculate pixel sizes, convert positions to array indices
3. **Initialization**: Initialize complex object (ones) and probe (scaled disk)
4. **Iterative Reconstruction**: ePIE algorithm with intensity constraint in Fourier domain
5. **Evaluation**: Align reconstruction to ground truth, compute PSNR/SSIM metrics

The algorithm reconstructs a complex-valued object and probe from intensity-only measurements at multiple overlapping scan positions.

task_43.name: PyAbel-master
task_43.title: Abel Inversion
task_43.description:
This code implements an **Abel Transform Inversion Pipeline** for reconstructing radially symmetric 3D density distributions from their 2D line-of-sight projections. The pipeline consists of:

1. **Data Preprocessing**: Load image, center using autoconvolution, enforce odd/square dimensions, extract symmetry-averaged quadrant
2. **Inversion**: Apply inverse Abel transform using Dasch Three-Point deconvolution method
3. **Forward Projection**: Apply forward Abel transform using Hansen-Law state-space method for validation
4. **Evaluation**: Compute reconstruction quality metrics (PSNR) comparing original projection to reprojection

The problem is a **linear inverse problem** where the forward operator is the Abel transform (radial integration along lines of sight).

task_44.name: pyDHM-master
task_44.title: Digital Holographic Microscopy
task_44.description:
This pipeline implements a **phase-shifting digital holographic microscopy (PS-DHM)** simulation and reconstruction system. The algorithm:

1. **Data Preprocessing**: Loads ground truth amplitude image, synthesizes phase, creates complex object field
2. **Forward Operator**: Simulates hologram recording with 4 phase-shifted reference waves using angular spectrum propagation
3. **Inversion**: Recovers complex field using PS4 algorithm and back-propagation
4. **Evaluation**: Computes PSNR and SSIM metrics comparing reconstructed vs ground truth amplitude

The core physics involves wave propagation via the Angular Spectrum Method (ASM) and four-step phase-shifting interferometry for complex field recovery.

task_45.name: PyHoloscope-main
task_45.title: Holoscope Reconstruction
task_45.description:
This code implements an **inline digital holography reconstruction pipeline** using the **Angular Spectrum Method (ASM)**. The pipeline consists of four stages:

1. **Data Preprocessing**: Load hologram/background images, perform background subtraction, optional windowing and downsampling
2. **Forward Operator**: Wave propagation using ASM transfer function in Fourier domain
3. **Inversion**: Back-propagation (adjoint operation) to reconstruct object plane from hologram
4. **Evaluation**: Self-consistency check by forward-projecting reconstruction and comparing to input

The reconstruction is a **direct inversion** (single-step back-propagation), not an iterative optimization.

task_46.name: PySMLFM-main
task_46.title: Single Molecule LFM
task_46.description:
This code implements a **Light Field Microscopy (LFM) 3D Reconstruction** algorithm that recovers 3D point positions from 2D localization measurements captured through a microlens array (MLA). The pipeline consists of:

1. **Data Generation/Preprocessing**: Generate synthetic 2D localizations from ground truth 3D points, assign localizations to MLA lenses, compute parallax vectors
2. **Forward Operator**: Physics-based projection model from 3D sample space to 2D sensor coordinates
3. **Inversion**: Iterative least-squares fitting to recover 3D positions from grouped 2D measurements
4. **Evaluation**: Match reconstructed points to ground truth and compute RMSE

The algorithm uses a **backward projection model** with least-squares optimization, iteratively grouping localizations by angular and disparity consistency before fitting.

metrics.eval_type: 3D localization accuracy (single molecule LFM)

task_47.name: spectral_ct_examples-master
task_47.title: Spectral CT Decomposition
task_47.description:
This code implements a **multi-material CT reconstruction** algorithm for spectral computed tomography. The pipeline reconstructs two material channels (bone/calcium and soft tissue) from correlated noisy projection data using a Douglas-Rachford Primal-Dual optimization framework with joint regularization.

**Pipeline Stages:**
1. Data loading and preprocessing (material combination, noise addition)
2. Noise covariance estimation and whitening transform construction
3. Forward operator setup (fan-beam CT with diagonal structure)
4. Inverse problem formulation with whitened data fidelity and nuclear norm regularization
5. Douglas-Rachford Primal-Dual optimization with FBP initialization
6. Quality evaluation via PSNR and SSIM metrics

task_48.name: storm-analysis-master
task_48.title: STORM Super-Resolution
task_48.description:
This code implements a **STORM (Stochastic Optical Reconstruction Microscopy) single-molecule localization** pipeline. The algorithm detects fluorescent spots in microscopy images and fits 2D symmetric Gaussians to precisely localize molecular positions. The pipeline consists of:

1. **Data Preprocessing**: Load DAX microscopy file and apply camera calibration (offset/gain correction)
2. **Peak Detection**: Use Difference-of-Gaussians (DoG) filtering with thresholding and local maxima detection
3. **Gaussian Fitting (Inversion)**: Fit 2D symmetric Gaussians to each detected peak using Levenberg-Marquardt optimization
4. **Forward Operator**: Reconstruct image from fitted Gaussian parameters
5. **Evaluation**: Compute PSNR between original and reconstructed images

task_49.name: structured-light-python-main
task_49.title: Structured Light 3D
task_49.description:
This pipeline implements a **stereo phase-shifting profilometry** system for 3D surface reconstruction. The algorithm:

1. **Data Preprocessing**: Generates multi-frequency sinusoidal fringe patterns for vertical and horizontal orientations
2. **Forward Operator**: Simulates stereo camera capture with known disparity shift
3. **Inversion**: Extracts wrapped phase, performs temporal unwrapping, matches correspondences via phase values, and triangulates 3D points
4. **Evaluation**: Computes reconstruction accuracy metrics against theoretical depth

The core physics model: `Z = (f × Baseline) / Disparity`

task_50.name: svmbir-master
task_50.title: Model-Based CT Reconstruction
task_50.description:
This code implements **Model-Based Image Reconstruction (MBIR)** for Computed Tomography (CT) using gradient descent with momentum and a Q-GGMRF (Q-Generalized Gaussian Markov Random Field) prior regularizer.

**Pipeline stages:**
1. `data_preprocessing`: Generate Shepp-Logan phantom, simulate sinogram via Radon transform, add noise
2. `forward_operator`: Radon transform (line integrals at specified angles)
3. `inversion`: Iterative reconstruction minimizing data fidelity + prior regularization
4. `evaluation`: Compute PSNR and SSIM metrics

task_51.name: tomopy-master
task_51.title: CT FBP/SIRT Reconstruction
task_51.description:
This code implements a Computed Tomography (CT) image reconstruction pipeline with the following stages:
1. **Data Acquisition Simulation**: Load phantom image, simulate X-ray CT acquisition with Poisson noise
2. **Forward Model**: Radon transform (line integrals at multiple angles)
3. **Reconstruction**: Two methods - Filtered Back Projection (FBP) and Simultaneous Iterative Reconstruction Technique (SIRT)
4. **Evaluation**: Compare reconstructions against ground truth using PSNR and SSIM metrics

The pipeline solves the inverse problem of recovering an image from its noisy sinogram measurements.

task_52.name: us-beamform-linarray-master
task_52.title: Ultrasound Beamforming
task_52.description:
This code implements an ultrasound B-mode image reconstruction pipeline for phased array imaging. The pipeline processes raw RF sensor data through preprocessing (time-gain compensation, filtering, interpolation, apodization), beamforming (fixed focus and dynamic focusing methods), and post-processing (envelope detection, log compression, scan conversion) to produce displayable grayscale ultrasound images. Quality metrics compare fixed-focus reconstruction against dynamic-focus reference.

**Pipeline Stages:**
1. Data loading from HDF5
2. Preprocessing (TGC, filtering, interpolation, apodization)
3. Beamforming (two methods: fixed focus, dynamic focus)
4. Post-processing (envelope detection, log compression, scan conversion)
5. Evaluation (PSNR, RMSE)

task_53.name: nirfaster-FF-main_2
task_53.title: Diffuse Optical Tomography
task_53.description:
This code implements a **Diffuse Optical Tomography (DOT) reconstruction pipeline** using the Finite Element Method (FEM) for solving the steady-state diffusion approximation and Tikhonov regularization for the inverse problem.

**Pipeline Stages:**
1. **Data Loading & Preprocessing**: Load mesh geometry, optical parameters, source/detector configurations; create synthetic anomaly; build mesh-to-grid interpolation
2. **Forward Problem**: Assemble FEM system matrix, solve for fluence fields, extract boundary measurements
3. **Jacobian Computation**: Use adjoint method to compute sensitivity matrix on a regular grid
4. **Inverse Problem**: Solve linearized reconstruction using Tikhonov regularization
5. **Evaluation**: Compute MSE and PSNR metrics, generate visualization

1. `data_generation`: Create synthetic ground-truth, run forward model, add noise
2. `forward_modeling`: Straight-ray travel time through 2D speed-of-sound map (ring geometry)
3. `inverse_solving`: LSQR tomographic inversion with damping
4. `evaluation`: Compute metrics (PSNR, SSIM, CC, RE), save results and figures

metrics.eval_type: Ultrasound computed tomography LSQR inversion (CC=0.858)

task_78.name: phoeniks
task_78.title: THz-TDS Parameter Extraction
task_78.description: This code extracts the complex refractive index n(ω) and extinction coefficient κ(ω) of a dielectric material from THz Time-Domain Spectroscopy (THz-TDS) measurements. The pipeline loads reference and sample THz time-domain traces, computes the experimental transfer function H(ω) = FFT(sample)/FFT(reference), then solves a frequency-by-frequency optimization to recover n and κ. Results are compared against known ground truth values for an artificial sample.

metrics.eval_type: 1D spectral fitting — THz-TDS parameter extraction (CC_n=0.99999813, CC_κ=0.99999806, CC_α=0.99999846)

task_79.name: openpiv
task_79.title: PIV Cross-Correlation
task_79.description:
This code implements a Particle Image Velocimetry (PIV) flow reconstruction pipeline using the OpenPIV library. The inverse problem recovers a 2D velocity field from a pair of synthetic particle images. A known velocity field (Lamb-Oseen vortex + uniform translation + strain) displaces tracer particles between two camera frames, and cross-correlation analysis in interrogation windows recovers the displacement (velocity) at each grid point.

**Pipeline**: Generate synthetic particle pair → Cross-correlation PIV → Outlier filtering → Velocity field reconstruction → Evaluate against ground truth.

Objective: `argmax_d CC(W_a(x), W_b(x+d))` where CC is cross-correlation, W_a and W_b are interrogation windows, d is displacement

metrics.eval_type: 2D velocity field reconstruction (PIV cross-correlation, CC_magnitude=0.998, AEE=0.189 px)

task_80.name: pymiescat
task_80.title: Mie Scattering Inversion
task_80.description:
This code implements aerosol particle refractive index and size inversion from multi-wavelength Mie scattering efficiency spectra. Given observed scattering (Qsca) and absorption (Qabs) efficiency spectra across UV-to-NIR wavelengths, the algorithm recovers the complex refractive index m = n + ik and particle diameter d using least-squares optimization against the Lorenz-Mie theory forward model.

**Pipeline**: Generate synthetic Mie spectra → Add noise → Differential evolution global search → L-BFGS-B refinement → Parameter recovery → Spectral evaluation.

Objective: `J(n,k,d) = Σ_λ [(Qsca_obs - Qsca_model)²/Qsca_obs² + (Qabs_obs - Qabs_model)²/Qabs_obs²]`

metrics.eval_type: 1D spectral parameter inversion (CC_Qext=0.999997, n_RE=0.04%, k_RE=0.34%, d_RE=0.27%)

task_81.name: rmtools
task_81.title: Faraday RM Synthesis
task_81.description: This code recovers the Faraday depth spectrum F(φ) from synthetic broadband radio polarization observations using RM synthesis followed by Hogbom CLEAN deconvolution. The forward model transforms a set of Faraday-thin polarized sources into complex polarization P(λ²) = Σ A_i exp(2iφ_iλ²), and the inverse algorithm (RM synthesis + CLEAN) recovers the Faraday depth positions and amplitudes of the sources.

metrics.eval_type: 1D Faraday depth spectral inversion (RM synthesis + CLEAN, peak_error=0.00 rad/m², amp_recovery=0.998)

task_82.name: pfsspy
task_82.title: Solar Coronal Magnetic Field
task_82.description: This code reconstructs the 3D coronal magnetic field from a synthetic photospheric magnetogram using the Potential Field Source Surface (PFSS) model. The forward model maps the 3D potential field B(r,θ,φ) to the photospheric radial component B_r(R_sun,θ,φ). The inverse algorithm solves Laplace's equation ∇²Ψ=0 with boundary conditions at the photosphere (B_r from magnetogram) and source surface (B_r=0, purely radial) via spherical harmonic decomposition.

metrics.eval_type: 2D coronal magnetic field reconstruction (PFSS model, RMSE=0.72 G, RE=0.20, flux_ratio=1.02)

task_83.name: radvel
task_83.title: Radial Velocity Exoplanet
task_83.description: This code recovers Keplerian orbital parameters (period, eccentricity, semi-amplitude, argument of periastron) of a 2-planet system from synthetic radial velocity time series. The forward model computes RV via the Keplerian equation v(t) = K[cos(ν(t)+ω) + e·cos(ω)] + γ, and the inverse algorithm performs maximum-likelihood fitting using Powell optimization to recover all orbital elements.

metrics.eval_type: 1D RV time series Keplerian orbit fitting (2-planet, RMS_res=3.32 m/s, χ²_red=1.19, Per1_err=0.01%, K1_err=1.79%)

task_84.name: blackhole_daps
task_84.title: Black Hole Imaging (DAPS)
task_84.description: This code reconstructs a black hole image from sparse radio interferometric (VLBI) observations using the DAPS (Decoupled Annealing Posterior Sampling) algorithm. The forward model maps a 64x64 image to complex visibilities, closure phases, and log closure amplitudes via EHT baseline sampling matrices. The inversion uses a score-based diffusion prior combined with annealed Langevin dynamics for data fidelity, iterating 200 annealing steps with 5-step reverse diffusion and 20-step Langevin correction at each level.

metrics.eval_type: 2D VLBI black hole image reconstruction (DAPS, cp_chi2=1.00, camp_chi2=1.27, blur_PSNR@15=38.82 dB)

task_85.name: blackhole_lgd
task_85.title: Black Hole Imaging (LGD)
task_85.description: This code reconstructs a black hole image from sparse VLBI observations using Likelihood Gradient Descent (LGD) guided diffusion. The forward model maps a 64x64 image to complex visibilities, closure phases, and log closure amplitudes via EHT baseline matrices. The inversion runs 1000-step VP-schedule reverse SDE with likelihood gradient guidance: at each step it denoises via the score network, computes the data-fidelity gradient through Monte Carlo samples of the posterior, and steers the diffusion trajectory toward measurement consistency.

metrics.eval_type: 2D VLBI black hole image reconstruction (LGD, cp_chi2=6.72, camp_chi2=5.75, blur_PSNR@15=17.08 dB)

task_86.name: cmaes_beeler_reuter_ap
task_86.title: Beeler-Reuter AP Estimation
task_86.description: This code recovers five log-transformed ionic conductance parameters (gNa, gNaC, gCa, gK1, gx1) of the Beeler-Reuter cardiac action potential model from noisy voltage and intracellular calcium time series. The forward model integrates an 8-state ODE system that maps conductance parameters to membrane voltage and [Ca2+]_i traces over 800 time points. The inversion uses CMA-ES (Covariance Matrix Adaptation Evolution Strategy) to minimize the weighted sum-of-squares residual between observed and predicted waveforms.

metrics.eval_type: ODE parameter estimation (CMA-ES, 5 params, weighted SSE=11.00, 214 iterations)

task_87.name: cmaes_hodgkin_huxley_ik
task_87.title: Hodgkin-Huxley IK Estimation
task_87.description: This code recovers five kinetic parameters of the Hodgkin-Huxley potassium current (IK) gating model from noisy voltage-clamp current recordings across 12 step potentials. The forward model analytically computes the gating variable n(t) for each voltage step using alpha/beta rate functions parameterized by (p1,...,p5), then calculates IK = g_max * n^4 * (V - E_K). The inversion uses CMA-ES to minimize the sum-of-squares residual between 4800 observed and predicted current samples.

metrics.eval_type: ODE parameter estimation (CMA-ES, 5 params, SSE=7.60e6, 323 iterations, p1_err=1.2%, p2_err=9.4%)

task_88.name: fwi_diffpir
task_88.title: Seismic FWI (DiffPIR)
task_88.description: This code reconstructs a 2D subsurface acoustic velocity model from multi-shot seismic waveform data using DiffPIR (Diffusion-based Plug-and-Play Image Restoration). The forward model solves the 2D acoustic wave equation via finite-difference time-domain simulation (Devito) to generate seismograms for multiple source positions. The inversion alternates between a pretrained score-based diffusion denoiser and data-fidelity gradient steps over 10 diffusion iterations, balancing prior consistency and measurement fit.

metrics.eval_type: 2D seismic velocity reconstruction (DiffPIR, rel_L2=0.110, data_misfit=143.3)

task_89.name: fwi_reddiff
task_89.title: Seismic FWI (REDDiff)
task_89.description: This code reconstructs a 2D subsurface acoustic velocity model from multi-shot seismic waveform data using REDDiff (Regularization by Denoising via Diffusion). The forward model solves the 2D acoustic wave equation via Devito FDTD simulation across multiple source-receiver configurations. The inversion uses a diffusion-based RED framework that alternates between score-network denoising and data-fidelity gradient descent over 10 diffusion steps, progressively refining the velocity estimate.

metrics.eval_type: 2D seismic velocity reconstruction (REDDiff, rel_L2=0.245, data_misfit=206.4)

task_90.name: inv-scatter_daps
task_90.title: Inverse Scattering (DAPS)
task_90.description: This code reconstructs a 2D scattering potential from far-field scattered wave measurements using DAPS (Decoupled Annealing Posterior Sampling). The forward model computes scattered fields from a 128x128 refractive index contrast map using the Born approximation and Hankel-function Green's functions for 20 transmitter and 360 receiver positions on a circular sensor array. The inversion uses DAPS with 200 annealing steps, combining a pretrained EDM diffusion prior with 100-step Langevin dynamics for data fidelity at each noise level.

metrics.eval_type: 2D inverse scattering potential reconstruction (DAPS, 128x128)

task_91.name: inv-scatter_ddnm
task_91.title: Inverse Scattering (DDNM)
task_91.description: This code reconstructs a 2D dielectric permittivity distribution from synthetic scattered electromagnetic field measurements using the DDNM (Denoising Diffusion Null-space Model) algorithm. The forward model simulates wave scattering via Green's functions with 20 transmitters and 360 receivers on a circular sensor array, mapping a 128x128 permittivity map to complex-valued scattered field data. The inversion leverages a pre-trained EDM diffusion prior and applies DDNM's range-null space decomposition (via SVD of the linearized forward operator) to enforce data consistency during the reverse diffusion process, projecting denoised estimates onto the measurement-consistent subspace at each step.

metrics.eval_type: 2D inverse scattering reconstruction (DDNM, 128x128 permittivity, PSNR=35.30 dB, SSIM=0.948)

task_92.name: inv-scatter_ddrm
task_92.title: Inverse Scattering (DDRM)
task_92.description: This code reconstructs a 2D dielectric permittivity map from scattered electromagnetic field measurements using DDRM (Denoising Diffusion Restoration Models). The forward model computes wave scattering via Hankel-function Green's functions with 20 transmitters and 360 receivers arranged on a circular array (128x128 grid). DDRM operates in the SVD spectral domain of the linearized forward operator, applying separate update rules for the signal and null-space components at each diffusion step, using a pre-trained EDM denoiser to guide the reconstruction while maintaining measurement fidelity.

metrics.eval_type: 2D inverse scattering reconstruction (DDRM, 128x128 permittivity, PSNR=32.52 dB, SSIM=0.932)

task_93.name: inv-scatter_diffpir
task_93.title: Inverse Scattering (DiffPIR)
task_93.description: This code reconstructs a 2D dielectric permittivity distribution from scattered electromagnetic field measurements using the DiffPIR (Denoising Diffusion Models for Plug-and-Play Image Restoration) algorithm. The forward model uses Green's function-based wave scattering simulation with 20 transmitters and 360 receivers on a circular array (128x128 grid). DiffPIR alternates between a data-fitting proximal step (enforcing consistency with measured scattered fields) and a diffusion-based denoising step using a pre-trained EDM model, iterating through 1000 diffusion timesteps to reconstruct the permittivity map.

metrics.eval_type: 2D inverse scattering reconstruction (DiffPIR, 128x128 permittivity, PSNR=7.67 dB, SSIM=0.064)

task_94.name: inv-scatter_dps
task_94.title: Inverse Scattering (DPS)
task_94.description: This code reconstructs a 2D dielectric permittivity distribution from scattered electromagnetic field measurements using DPS (Diffusion Posterior Sampling). The forward model simulates wave scattering via Green's functions with 20 transmitters and 360 receivers on a circular array (128x128 grid). DPS modifies the reverse diffusion process by adding a likelihood gradient term at each step: it computes the gradient of the data-fidelity loss (squared error between predicted and observed scattered fields) with respect to the denoised estimate, and uses this gradient to steer sampling toward the posterior distribution conditioned on the measurements.

metrics.eval_type: 2D inverse scattering reconstruction (DPS, 128x128 permittivity, PSNR=14.66 dB, SSIM=0.498)

task_95.name: inv-scatter_fps
task_95.title: Inverse Scattering (FPS)
task_95.description: This code reconstructs a 2D dielectric permittivity distribution from scattered electromagnetic field measurements using the FPS (Filtered Posterior Sampling) algorithm. The forward model simulates wave scattering via Green's functions with 20 transmitters and 360 receivers on a circular array (128x128 grid). FPS combines the SVD decomposition of the linearized forward operator with a pre-trained EDM diffusion prior, performing filtered posterior sampling in the spectral domain to produce multiple posterior samples. It generates 20 posterior samples and averages them for the final reconstruction.

metrics.eval_type: 2D inverse scattering reconstruction (FPS, 128x128 permittivity, 20 samples, PSNR=30.85 dB, SSIM=0.782)

task_96.name: inv-scatter_lgd
task_96.title: Inverse Scattering (LGD)
task_96.description: This code reconstructs a 2D dielectric permittivity distribution from scattered electromagnetic field measurements using LGD (Latent Gradient Descent with diffusion prior). The forward model simulates wave scattering via Green's functions with 20 transmitters and 360 receivers on a circular array (128x128 grid). LGD performs gradient descent on the data-fidelity loss in the latent space of a pre-trained EDM diffusion model, iterating through 1000 steps to optimize the reconstruction while leveraging the learned prior distribution for regularization.

metrics.eval_type: 2D inverse scattering reconstruction (LGD, 128x128 permittivity, PSNR=7.71 dB, SSIM=0.182)

task_97.name: inv-scatter_mcgdiff
task_97.title: Inverse Scattering (MCGDiff)
task_97.description: This code reconstructs a 2D dielectric permittivity distribution from scattered electromagnetic field measurements using MCGDiff (Monte Carlo Guided Diffusion). The forward model simulates wave scattering via Green's functions with 20 transmitters and 360 receivers on a circular array (128x128 grid). MCGDiff uses an SVD-based decomposition of the forward operator combined with a pre-trained EDM diffusion prior, running particle-based Monte Carlo sampling during the reverse diffusion process to approximate the posterior distribution. It uses importance weighting across multiple particles to guide the sampling toward measurement-consistent reconstructions over 200 diffusion steps.

metrics.eval_type: 2D inverse scattering reconstruction (MCGDiff, 128x128 permittivity, PSNR=30.43 dB, SSIM=0.778)

task_98.name: inv-scatter_pigdm
task_98.title: Inverse Scattering (PiGDM)
task_98.description: This code reconstructs a 2D scattering potential from multi-static scattered electromagnetic field measurements using the PiGDM (Pseudo-inverse Guided Diffusion Model) algorithm. The forward model simulates wave scattering via Green's functions and Hankel functions, mapping a 128x128 permittivity contrast image to complex-valued scattered fields measured at 360 receivers for 20 transmitter positions. The inversion uses a pretrained score-based diffusion prior with pseudo-inverse guidance for data consistency, iterating through 100 reverse diffusion steps with a sqrt noise schedule to recover the scattering potential.

metrics.eval_type: 2D inverse scattering reconstruction (PiGDM, 128x128, 20 transmitters, 360 receivers)

task_99.name: inv-scatter_pnpdm
task_99.title: Inverse Scattering (PnPDM)
task_99.description: This code reconstructs a 2D scattering potential from multi-static scattered electromagnetic field measurements using the PnPDM (Plug-and-Play Diffusion Model) algorithm. The forward model simulates electromagnetic wave scattering via Green's functions and Hankel functions, mapping a 128x128 permittivity contrast to complex scattered fields at 360 receivers for 20 transmitters. The inversion alternates between Langevin gradient descent steps for data consistency (using the physics-based forward model) and reverse diffusion denoising steps via a pretrained score-based prior, using an exponentially decaying annealing schedule over 10 outer iterations.

metrics.eval_type: 2D inverse scattering reconstruction (PnPDM, 128x128, 20 transmitters, 360 receivers)

task_100.name: inv-scatter_reddiff
task_100.title: Inverse Scattering (REDDiff)
task_100.description: This code reconstructs a 2D scattering potential from multi-static scattered electromagnetic field measurements using the REDDiff (Regularization by Denoising with Diffusion) algorithm. The forward model simulates electromagnetic wave scattering via Green's functions and Hankel functions, mapping a 128x128 permittivity contrast to complex scattered fields at 360 receivers for 20 transmitters. The inversion optimizes a variational objective combining a data-fidelity term (forward model residual) with a denoising-based regularizer from a pretrained diffusion model, using Adam with VP noise schedule over 10 optimization steps.

metrics.eval_type: 2D inverse scattering reconstruction (REDDiff, 128x128, 20 transmitters, 360 receivers)

task_101.name: mcmc_fitzhugh_nagumo
task_101.title: FitzHugh-Nagumo MCMC Estimation
task_101.description: This code estimates parameters of the FitzHugh-Nagumo neuron model from noisy voltage and recovery variable time series. The forward model integrates the coupled ODE system dV/dt = c(V - V^3/3 + R), dR/dt = -(V - a + bR)/c to produce 2D time series given parameters (a, b, c). The inversion first performs CMA-ES optimization to find a point estimate, then runs Monomial-Gamma Hamiltonian Monte Carlo (4 chains, 20 iterations) with a Gaussian likelihood and uniform prior to sample the posterior distribution over 5 parameters (a, b, c, sigma_V, sigma_R).

metrics.eval_type: ODE parameter estimation (FitzHugh-Nagumo, CMA-ES + MCMC, a_mean=0.09 vs 0.10, b_mean=0.46 vs 0.50, c_mean=3.10 vs 3.00)

task_102.name: mcmc_goodwin_oscillator
task_102.title: Goodwin Oscillator MCMC
task_102.description: This code estimates parameters of the Goodwin oscillator model for gene expression dynamics from noisy 3-state time series. The forward model integrates a 3-state ODE system with Hill-function nonlinearity and linear degradation, parameterized by (k2, k3, m1, m2, m3). The inversion uses XNES optimization for point estimation, followed by Relativistic MCMC (4 chains, 20 iterations) with Gaussian likelihood and uniform prior to sample the posterior over 8 parameters (5 model + 3 noise). The MCMC posterior means closely match the true parameters (k2=2.05 vs 2.0, k3=4.03 vs 4.0, m1=0.12 vs 0.12, m2=0.08 vs 0.08, m3=0.10 vs 0.10).

metrics.eval_type: ODE parameter estimation (Goodwin oscillator, XNES + MCMC, k2=2.05/2.0, k3=4.03/4.0, m1=0.12/0.12, m2=0.08/0.08, m3=0.10/0.10)

task_103.name: mcmc_hes1_michaelis_menten
task_103.title: Hes1 Michaelis-Menten MCMC
task_103.description: This code estimates kinetic parameters of the Hes1 gene expression oscillator from noisy concentration time series. The forward model integrates a 3-state ODE system with Michaelis-Menten repression (Hill function nonlinearity) governed by parameters (P0, nu, k1, h), where P0 is the repression threshold, h is the Hill coefficient, and k1 and nu are rate constants. The inversion uses both Haario-Bardenet adaptive covariance MCMC (3 chains, 1000 iterations) and Hamiltonian Monte Carlo (3 chains, 10 iterations) with Gaussian likelihood and log-uniform prior to sample the posterior over 5 parameters (4 model + 1 noise sigma).

metrics.eval_type: ODE parameter estimation (Hes1 Michaelis-Menten, Adaptive MCMC + HMC, P0_mean=2.79, nu_mean=0.03, k1_mean=0.30, h_mean=7.79)

task_104.name: mcmc_lotka_volterra
task_104.title: Lotka-Volterra MCMC
task_104.description: This code estimates parameters of the Lotka-Volterra predator-prey model from noisy population time series (hare-lynx data). The forward model integrates the coupled ODE system dx/dt = ax - bxy, dy/dt = -cy + dxy, mapping 4 kinetic parameters (a, b, c, d) to 2D population dynamics. The inversion uses both Haario-Bardenet adaptive covariance MCMC (4 chains, 400 iterations) and Hamiltonian Monte Carlo (4 chains, 20 iterations) with Gaussian likelihood and uniform prior to sample the posterior over 6 parameters (4 model + 2 noise sigmas).

metrics.eval_type: ODE parameter estimation (Lotka-Volterra, Adaptive MCMC + HMC, a=0.41, b=0.17, c=0.98, d=0.30)

task_105.name: mcmc_repressilator
task_105.title: Repressilator MCMC Inference
task_105.description: This code performs Bayesian parameter estimation for the repressilator gene regulatory network model using MCMC sampling. The forward model simulates a 6-state ODE system (3 mRNA + 3 protein concentrations) governed by 4 parameters (alpha_0, alpha, beta, Hill coefficient n) via scipy's odeint. The inverse algorithm uses Haario-Bardenet Adaptive Covariance MCMC with 3 parallel chains to sample the posterior distribution of the kinetic parameters from noisy time-series observations of mRNA concentrations.

metrics.eval_type: ODE parameter estimation via MCMC (4 params: alpha_0, alpha, beta, n; rhat=[1.49, 9.26, 2.32, 1.22], ESS=[21.5, 14.3, 14.6, 13.6])

task_106.name: mcmc_sir
task_106.title: SIR Epidemic MCMC Inference
task_106.description: This code performs Bayesian parameter estimation for the SIR (Susceptible-Infected-Recovered) epidemiological model using MCMC sampling. The forward model solves a 3-compartment ODE system dS/dt=-gamma*S*I, dI/dt=gamma*S*I-v*I, dR/dt=v*I with parameters gamma (infection rate), v (recovery rate), and S0 (initial susceptibles). The inverse algorithm uses Haario-Bardenet Adaptive Covariance MCMC with 3 chains and Gaussian likelihood to infer the 3 epidemiological parameters plus 2 noise standard deviations from observed infection/recovery time-series data (Common Cold outbreak on Tristan da Cunha).

metrics.eval_type: ODE parameter estimation via MCMC (SIR model, 5 params: gamma, v, S0, sigma_I, sigma_R; rhat=[1.20, 1.25, 1.38, 1.16, 1.31])

task_107.name: mri_L1
task_107.title: MRI Compressed Sensing (L1)
task_107.description: This code reconstructs a 2D knee MRI image from undersampled multi-coil k-space data using L1 wavelet compressed sensing. The forward model applies coil sensitivity maps to the image, performs 2D FFT, and applies a random 4x undersampling mask in k-space. The inverse algorithm uses iterative soft-thresholding (ISTA) with L1 wavelet regularization (lambda=1e-6) via SigPy's L1WaveletRecon to recover the 320x320 complex-valued MRI image from the undersampled multi-coil measurements.

metrics.eval_type: 2D multi-coil MRI reconstruction (L1 Wavelet CS, 4x acceleration, data_misfit=29.88)

task_108.name: mri_TV
task_108.title: MRI Compressed Sensing (TV)
task_108.description: This code reconstructs a 2D knee MRI image from undersampled multi-coil k-space data using Total Variation (TV) compressed sensing. The forward model applies coil sensitivity maps to the image, performs 2D FFT, and applies a random 4x undersampling mask in k-space. The inverse algorithm uses proximal gradient descent with TV regularization (lambda=1e-6) via SigPy's TotalVariationRecon to recover the 320x320 complex-valued MRI image, promoting piecewise-smooth solutions.

metrics.eval_type: 2D multi-coil MRI reconstruction (TV CS, 4x acceleration, data_misfit=30.64)

task_109.name: mri_csgm_mri
task_109.title: MRI CSGM Reconstruction
task_109.description: This code reconstructs a 2D knee MRI image from undersampled multi-coil k-space data using CSGM (Compressed Sensing using Generative Models). The forward model applies coil sensitivity maps, 2D FFT, and a random 4x undersampling mask. The inverse algorithm uses a pretrained EDM (Elucidating Diffusion Models) UNet as a score-based prior combined with Langevin dynamics: it iteratively updates the reconstruction by balancing a data fidelity gradient (computed via the forward operator) and a prior score from the diffusion model across a geometric noise schedule of 2311 sigma levels.

metrics.eval_type: 2D multi-coil MRI reconstruction (CSGM diffusion prior, 4x acceleration, data_misfit=64.88)

task_110.name: mri_daps
task_110.title: MRI DAPS Reconstruction
task_110.description: This code reconstructs a 2D knee MRI image from undersampled multi-coil k-space data using DAPS (Decoupled Annealing Posterior Sampling). The forward model applies coil sensitivity maps, 2D FFT, and a random 4x undersampling mask. The inverse algorithm alternates three stages per annealing step: (1) reverse diffusion using a pretrained EDM UNet to denoise from current noise level, (2) Langevin dynamics with 100 gradient steps to enforce data consistency via the forward operator, and (3) forward diffusion to re-noise at the next annealing level. This decoupled approach runs 200 annealing steps with a polynomial-7 sigma schedule from sigma=100 to 0.1.

metrics.eval_type: 2D multi-coil MRI reconstruction (DAPS, 4x acceleration, data_misfit=31.38)

task_111.name: mri_diffpir
task_111.title: MRI DiffPIR Reconstruction
task_111.description: This code reconstructs a 2D knee MRI image from undersampled multi-coil k-space data using DiffPIR (Denoising Diffusion Models for Plug-and-Play Image Restoration). The forward model applies coil sensitivity maps, 2D FFT, and a random 4x undersampling mask. The inverse algorithm iterates through a diffusion noise schedule: at each step it (1) denoises xt using a pretrained EDM UNet to get x0, (2) computes a data fidelity gradient via the forward operator with adaptive regularization rho proportional to sigma^2, and (3) updates x0hat by gradient descent on data consistency, then re-noises to the next sigma level with a stochasticity parameter xi.

metrics.eval_type: 2D multi-coil MRI reconstruction (DiffPIR, 4x acceleration, data_misfit=176.09)

task_112.name: mri_dps
task_112.title: MRI Reconstruction (DPS)
task_112.description: This code reconstructs multi-coil MRI knee images from undersampled k-space data using Diffusion Posterior Sampling (DPS). The forward model applies coil sensitivity maps to a complex-valued image and computes masked Fourier (k-space) measurements with 4x random acceleration. The inversion uses a pretrained score-based diffusion model (EDM UNet, VP schedule) as a prior, guiding reverse diffusion steps with data-consistency gradients computed through the forward operator to recover the latent MR image.

metrics.eval_type: 2D multi-coil MRI reconstruction (DPS, 4x acceleration, data_misfit=31.34)

task_113.name: mri_pnpdm
task_113.title: MRI Reconstruction (PnPDM)
task_113.description: This code reconstructs multi-coil MRI knee images from undersampled k-space using Plug-and-Play Diffusion Models (PnPDM). The forward model maps a complex MR image through coil sensitivity maps and masked FFT to produce k-space observations with 4x random undersampling. The inversion alternates between Langevin gradient dynamics for data fidelity (using the forward operator gradient) and reverse diffusion sampling with a pretrained denoiser, following an exponential annealing schedule across noise levels.

metrics.eval_type: 2D multi-coil MRI reconstruction (PnPDM, 4x acceleration, divergent reconstruction)

task_114.name: mri_reddiff
task_114.title: MRI Reconstruction (REDDiff)
task_114.description: This code reconstructs multi-coil MRI knee images from 4x-accelerated k-space using Regularization by Denoising with Diffusion (REDDiff).
