The inversion iteratively optimizes a proximal objective that balances a data-fidelity term (via the forward operator) with a denoiser-based regularization prior, using a pretrained EDM score network across a linear noise schedule.

metrics.eval_type: 2D multi-coil MRI reconstruction (REDDiff, 4x acceleration, data_misfit=80.28)

task_115.name: mri_score_mri
task_115.title: MRI Reconstruction (ScoreMRI)
task_115.description: This code reconstructs multi-coil MRI knee images from 4x randomly undersampled k-space using ScoreMRI, a score-based diffusion method. The forward model applies coil sensitivity maps followed by masked FFT to simulate accelerated MRI acquisition. The inversion performs 2000 predictor-corrector steps through the VP reverse SDE, incorporating data-consistency updates via Langevin correction with the forward operator gradient at each diffusion step to enforce measurement fidelity.

metrics.eval_type: 2D multi-coil MRI reconstruction (ScoreMRI, 4x acceleration, data_misfit=37.72)

task_116.name: multireflection_bcdi_SiC
task_116.title: Multi-Reflection BCDI (SiC)
task_116.description: This code reconstructs the 3D electron density and lattice displacement field of a SiC (silicon carbide) crystal from multi-reflection Bragg Coherent Diffraction Imaging (BCDI) data. The forward model computes 3D diffraction patterns by applying displacement-dependent phase shifts to a complex object and performing 3D FFT for each Bragg reflection. The inversion uses Adam optimization with total variation regularization in two phases: Phase 1 recovers electron density and displacement jointly with minibatch stochastic updates, and Phase 2 refines the displacement field with a restricted amplitude mask and median filtering.

metrics.eval_type: 3D BCDI reconstruction (SiC, 6 reflections, 2-phase Adam optimizer with TV regularization)

task_117.name: multireflection_bcdi_adam_nodisloc
task_117.title: BCDI Adam (No Dislocation)
task_117.description: This code reconstructs the 3D electron density and lattice displacement field of a dislocation-free Au (gold) nanocrystal from multi-reflection BCDI data with 5 Bragg reflections. The forward model computes simulated diffraction amplitudes via displacement-dependent phase modulation and 3D FFT for each reflection, with Poisson-stabilized intensity matching. The inversion uses Adam optimization with TV regularization in two phases: Phase 1 jointly recovers amplitude and displacement with minibatch training, and Phase 2 refines displacement on a restricted amplitude support with lower learning rate.

metrics.eval_type: 3D BCDI reconstruction (Au no-dislocation, final_loss=2.31e+01, amplitude_range=[0.989,0.991], displacement_range=[-0.18,0.19] nm)

task_118.name: multireflection_bcdi_adam_twodisloc
task_118.title: BCDI Adam (Two Dislocations)
task_118.description: This code reconstructs the 3D electron density and lattice displacement field of an Au (gold) nanocrystal containing two screw dislocations from multi-reflection BCDI data with 4 Bragg reflections. The forward model simulates coherent diffraction amplitudes via displacement-induced phase shifts on a complex object field followed by 3D FFT per reflection. The inversion uses Adam optimization with TV regularization and median filtering in two phases: Phase 1 recovers the full object with progressive minibatch scheduling, and Phase 2 refines displacement on a thresholded amplitude support.

metrics.eval_type: 3D BCDI reconstruction (Au two-dislocation, 4 reflections, 2-phase Adam with TV regularization and median filtering)

task_119.name: navier_stokes_dpg
task_119.title: Navier-Stokes DPG Inversion
task_119.description: This code recovers a 2D vorticity field governed by the Navier-Stokes equations from sparse, noisy, time-evolved observations using the Diffusion Posterior Gradient (DPG) algorithm. The forward model applies a spectral Navier-Stokes solver to propagate an initial vorticity field forward in time, then downsamples and adds Gaussian noise. The inversion leverages a pretrained EDM diffusion prior and iteratively refines samples via score-based guidance with Monte-Carlo reward shaping, optimizing data-fitting loss through stochastic gradient updates over 100 diffusion steps on a 128x128 grid.

metrics.eval_type: 2D vorticity field reconstruction (DPG, relative_L2=0.869)

task_120.name: navier_stokes_enkg
task_120.title: Navier-Stokes EnKG Inversion
task_120.description: This code recovers a 2D vorticity field from downsampled, noisy Navier-Stokes time-evolved observations using the Ensemble Kalman Gradient (EnKG) algorithm. The forward model propagates an initial vorticity field via a spectral Navier-Stokes solver, downsamples spatially, and adds noise. The inversion maintains an ensemble of particles guided by a pretrained EDM diffusion model, updating them through ODE sampling and Kalman-type gradient corrections that match forward-modeled measurements to observed data over 80 diffusion steps on a 128x128 grid.

metrics.eval_type: 2D vorticity field reconstruction (EnKG, relative_L2=0.586)

task_121.name: navier_stokes_scg
task_121.title: Navier-Stokes SCG Inversion
task_121.description: This code recovers a 2D vorticity field from downsampled, noisy Navier-Stokes time-evolved observations using the Stochastic Candidate Guidance (SCG) algorithm. The forward model applies a spectral Navier-Stokes solver to propagate vorticity forward in time, downsamples, and adds noise. The inversion uses a pretrained EDM diffusion model with a candidate selection strategy: at each diffusion step, multiple candidate next-states are generated and the one with lowest data-fitting loss (computed via the forward operator) is selected, over 10 diffusion steps on a 128x128 grid.

metrics.eval_type: 2D vorticity field reconstruction (SCG, relative_L2=1.672)

task_122.name: tomo_fbp_cpu
task_122.title: Tomography FBP CPU
task_122.description: This code reconstructs a 2D tomographic cross-section from sinogram data using Filtered Back Projection (FBP) on CPU via the ASTRA Toolbox. The forward model is the Radon transform (parallel-beam geometry), mapping a 2D attenuation image to a sinogram of 1125 projection angles over 924 detector pixels. The inversion applies FBP, a direct analytical method that filters each sinogram row in the frequency domain and backprojects to recover the image. Evaluation uses SSIM, NRMSE, and CNR against a 50-iteration SIRT reference.

metrics.eval_type: 2D CT reconstruction (FBP CPU, SSIM=0.472, NRMSE=0.777, CNR=5.90)

task_123.name: tomo_fbp_gpu
task_123.title: Tomography FBP GPU (CUDA)
task_123.description: This code reconstructs a 2D tomographic cross-section from sinogram data using Filtered Back Projection (FBP) accelerated on GPU (FBP_CUDA) via the ASTRA Toolbox. The forward model is the parallel-beam Radon transform mapping a 924x924 attenuation image to a sinogram of 1125 angles. The inversion applies GPU-accelerated FBP with a Hamming filter for direct analytical reconstruction. Evaluation computes SSIM, NRMSE, and CNR against a 200-iteration SIRT_CUDA reference, with forward projection used for residual validation.

metrics.eval_type: 2D CT reconstruction (FBP GPU, SSIM=0.929, NRMSE=0.194, CNR=11.77)

task_124.name: tomo_nnfbp
task_124.title: Tomography NN-FBP
task_124.description: This code reconstructs a 2D tomographic cross-section from sparse-angle sinogram data using Neural Network Filtered Back Projection (NN-FBP). The forward model is the parallel-beam Radon transform via ASTRA. The inversion trains a shallow neural network (3 hidden nodes, Levenberg-Marquardt optimization) to learn optimal reconstruction filters from high-quality FBP references, then applies these learned filters via custom filtered backprojection with sigmoid nonlinearity. Training uses 5 slices with 5000 random pixel samples per slice, with every 3rd projection angle for sparse reconstruction.

metrics.eval_type: 2D CT reconstruction (NN-FBP, SSIM=0.856, NRMSE=0.247, CNR=20.03)

task_125.name: tomo_sirt_gpu
task_125.title: Tomography SIRT GPU
task_125.description: This code reconstructs a 2D tomographic cross-section from sinogram data using the Simultaneous Iterative Reconstruction Technique (SIRT) on GPU via the ASTRA Toolbox. The forward model is the parallel-beam Radon transform mapping a 924x924 attenuation image to a sinogram of 1125 angles. The inversion applies SIRT, an iterative algebraic method: x^{k+1} = x^k + C * A^T * R * (b - A*x^k), where R and C are row/column normalization matrices. Runs 100 iterations with GPU-accelerated forward/back projection. Evaluated with SSIM, NRMSE, and CNR against a 200-iteration reference.

metrics.eval_type: 2D CT reconstruction (SIRT GPU, SSIM=0.972, NRMSE=0.104, CNR=31.22)

task_126.name: unmixing_AA_DC1
task_126.title: Hyperspectral Unmixing (AA)
task_126.description: This code performs blind hyperspectral unmixing on the DC1 synthetic dataset using Archetypal Analysis (AA). The forward model is the linear mixing model Y = E·A, where E (L×p) contains endmember spectra and A (p×N) contains fractional abundances per pixel. The inversion uses the SPAMS archetypal analysis solver, which jointly estimates endmembers and abundances by decomposing observed spectra as convex combinations of extreme points (archetypes) in the data simplex. The 5-endmember problem operates on 224-band hyperspectral data (75×75 pixels) with SNR=30 dB additive Gaussian noise.

metrics.eval_type: Blind hyperspectral unmixing (Archetypal Analysis, SRE=7.18 dB, aRMSE=10.47%, SAD=1.78°, eRMSE=4.11%)

task_127.name: unmixing_ADMMNet_DC1
task_127.title: Hyperspectral Unmixing (ADMMNet)
task_127.description: This code performs blind hyperspectral unmixing on the DC1 dataset using ADMMNet, a deep unrolling network that unfolds the Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM) optimization into a learnable neural architecture. The forward model is the linear mixing model Y = E·A. The network consists of X-blocks (linear system solve), Z-blocks (soft-thresholding), and D-blocks (dual variable update) with learnable parameters. VCA initializes endmembers, and the model is trained end-to-end via Adam optimizer with MSE loss on 224-band, 75×75 pixel data with 5 endmembers.

metrics.eval_type: Blind hyperspectral unmixing (ADMMNet, SRE=10.74 dB, aRMSE=6.95%, SAD=0.80°, eRMSE=1.53%)

task_128.name: unmixing_CLSUnSAL_DC1
task_128.title: Hyperspectral Unmixing (CLSUnSAL)
task_128.description: This code performs semi-supervised hyperspectral unmixing on the DC1 dataset using Collaborative Sparse Unmixing via variable Splitting and Augmented Lagrangian (CLSUnSAL). The forward model is Y = D·X, where D (L×M) is a known spectral dictionary of 240 atoms and X (M×N) are sparse abundance coefficients. The inversion uses an ADMM-based algorithm enforcing collaborative row-sparsity (group lasso), abundance non-negativity, and data fidelity constraints. Only abundance estimation is performed (endmembers come from the dictionary).

metrics.eval_type: Semi-supervised hyperspectral unmixing (CLSUnSAL, SRE=8.56 dB, aRMSE=8.94%)

task_129.name: unmixing_CNNAEU_DC1
task_129.title: Hyperspectral Unmixing (CNNAEU)
task_129.description: This code performs blind hyperspectral unmixing on the DC1 dataset using a CNN-based Autoencoder for Unmixing (CNNAEU). The forward model is the linear mixing model Y = E·A. The encoder is a convolutional network (Conv2d layers with LeakyReLU, BatchNorm, Dropout) that maps image patches to softmax-normalized abundance maps, and the decoder is a single Conv2d layer whose weights represent endmember spectra. Training minimizes spectral angle distance (SAD) loss on random patches extracted from the 224-band, 75×75 pixel image with 5 endmembers.

metrics.eval_type: Blind hyperspectral unmixing (CNNAEU, SRE=1.40 dB, aRMSE=20.37%, SAD=8.70°, eRMSE=74.34%)

task_130.name: unmixing_DS_DC1
task_130.title: Hyperspectral Unmixing (DecompSimplex)
task_130.description: This code performs supervised hyperspectral unmixing on the DC1 dataset using VCA for endmember extraction followed by DecompSimplex for abundance estimation. The forward model is the linear mixing model Y = E·A. VCA (Vertex Component Analysis) identifies pure pixel endmembers via geometric projections in the spectral simplex, then SPAMS DecompSimplex solves for non-negative abundances that sum to one via simplex-constrained decomposition. The pipeline processes 224-band, 75×75 pixel data with 5 endmembers at SNR=30 dB.

metrics.eval_type: Supervised hyperspectral unmixing (VCA+DecompSimplex, SRE=5.54 dB, aRMSE=12.65%, SAD=1.20°, eRMSE=4.35%)

task_131.name: unmixing_EDAA_DC1
task_131.title: Hyperspectral Unmixing (EDAA)
task_131.description: This code performs blind hyperspectral unmixing on the DC1 dataset using Entropic Descent Archetypal Analysis (EDAA). The forward model is Y ≈ (Y·B)·A, where B (N×p) is a selection matrix and A (p×N) are abundances, with endmembers E = Y·B expressed as convex combinations of observed spectra. The inversion uses entropic mirror descent with softmax updates to jointly optimize B and A over multiple random initializations, selecting the best result based on L1 residual and endmember correlation. Operates on 224-band, 75×75 pixel data with 5 endmembers.

metrics.eval_type: Blind hyperspectral unmixing (EDAA, SRE=5.20 dB, aRMSE=13.15%, SAD=8.17°, eRMSE=16.32%)

task_132.name: unmixing_MSNet_DC1
task_132.title: Hyperspectral Unmixing (MSNet)
task_132.description: This code performs blind hyperspectral unmixing on the DC1 dataset using MSNet, a multi-scale convolutional neural network. The forward model is the linear mixing model Y = E·A, implemented via 1×1 Conv2d decoder layers at three spatial scales. The encoder processes the image at 1×, 2×, and 4× downsampled resolutions with skip connections, producing softmax-normalized abundance maps. The loss combines SAD reconstruction loss, MSE loss, and Laplacian edge-preserving regularization across all scales. VCA initializes decoder weights. Trained on 224-band, 75×75 pixel data with 5 endmembers.

metrics.eval_type: Blind hyperspectral unmixing (MSNet, SRE=6.97 dB, aRMSE=10.73%, SAD=1.20°, eRMSE=4.35%)

task_133.name: unmixing_MiSiCNet_DC1
task_133.title: Hyperspectral Unmixing (MiSiCNet)
task_133.description: This code performs blind hyperspectral unmixing on synthetic DC1 data using MiSiCNet, a CNN-based deep image prior approach. The forward model is a linear mixing model Y = E·A where E (L×p endmember matrix) and A (p×N abundance matrix) are both unknown. The inversion uses a convolutional neural network with skip connections that takes random noise as input and outputs abundance maps, while endmembers are learned as a linear decoder layer. Optimization is performed via Adam with 800 iterations, enforcing non-negativity and sum-to-one constraints on abundances via softmax, and physical bounds [0,1] on endmembers. Initial endmembers are extracted using SiVM (Simplex Volume Maximization).

metrics.eval_type: Blind hyperspectral unmixing (MiSiCNet, SRE=6.24 dB, aRMSE=11.66, SAD=1.72°, eRMSE=3.78)

task_134.name: unmixing_PGMSU_DC1
task_134.title: Hyperspectral Unmixing (PGMSU)
task_134.description: This code performs blind hyperspectral unmixing on synthetic DC1 data using PGMSU (Probabilistic Generative Model for Spectral Unmixing), a VAE-based deep learning approach. The forward model is a linear mixing model Y = A·E where pixel-specific endmembers E are generated by a decoder network from a latent variable z, and abundances A are estimated by a separate encoder branch with softmax output. The inversion jointly trains a variational autoencoder: the z-encoder learns a latent representation, the decoder generates per-pixel endmember spectra, and the abundance encoder estimates fractional abundances. Training uses reconstruction loss, KL divergence regularization, VCA-guided warm-start for the first half of epochs, then minimum-volume and SAD regularization for the second half. Optimization uses Adam over 200 epochs with mini-batch training.

metrics.eval_type: Blind hyperspectral unmixing (PGMSU, SRE=10.08 dB, aRMSE=7.50, SAD=2.39°, eRMSE=4.72)

task_135.name: unmixing_S2WSU_DC1
task_135.title: Sparse Unmixing (S2WSU)
task_135.description: This code performs semi-supervised sparse hyperspectral unmixing on synthetic DC1 data using S2WSU (Spatially-Weighted Sparse Unmixing). The forward model is a linear mixing model Y = D·x where D is a known spectral dictionary (L×M) and x is a sparse coefficient matrix (M×N) representing fractional abundances over dictionary atoms. The inversion uses an ADMM (Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers) optimization with spatially-weighted L1 sparsity regularization, where weights are computed from local spatial neighborhoods using a 3×3 convolution kernel to promote spatial smoothness. The algorithm alternates between proximal steps for data fidelity, non-negativity, and weighted soft-thresholding for sparsity, with iterative reweighting of the spatial prior across outer loops.

metrics.eval_type: Semi-supervised sparse unmixing (S2WSU, SRE=9.34 dB, aRMSE=8.16)

task_136.name: unmixing_SUnAA_DC1
task_136.title: Unmixing via Archetypal Analysis (SUnAA)
task_136.description: This code performs semi-supervised hyperspectral unmixing on synthetic DC1 data using SUnAA (Semi-supervised Unmixing with Archetypal Analysis). The forward model is a two-level linear mixing model Y = (D·B)·A, where D is a known spectral dictionary (L×M), B is a coefficient matrix (M×p) mapping dictionary atoms to p endmembers as convex combinations, and A is the abundance matrix (p×N). The inversion alternates between: (1) updating B by solving simplex-constrained subproblems per endmember using SPAMS, and (2) updating A by projecting onto the simplex via SPAMS decompSimplex. This block-coordinate descent ensures endmembers are reconstructed as convex combinations of library spectra while abundances satisfy sum-to-one and non-negativity constraints.

metrics.eval_type: Semi-supervised unmixing via archetypal analysis (SUnAA, SRE=21.42 dB, aRMSE=2.03)

task_137.name: unmixing_SUnCNN_DC1
task_137.title: Unmixing via Deep Prior (SUnCNN)
task_137.description: This code performs semi-supervised hyperspectral unmixing on synthetic DC1 data using SUnCNN (Semi-supervised Unmixing with Convolutional Neural Network). The forward model is a linear mixing model Y = D·A where D is a known spectral dictionary (L×M) and A is the abundance matrix (M×N). The inversion uses a CNN-based deep image prior: a convolutional encoder network with skip connections, batch normalization, and LeakyReLU activations takes random noise as input and outputs softmax-normalized abundance maps. The network is trained by minimizing the MSE between observed spectra Y and predicted spectra D·A(θ), with exponential moving averaging of abundance estimates for stability. Optimization uses Adam for 2000 iterations (reduced from 20000 for efficiency).

metrics.eval_type: Semi-supervised unmixing via deep prior (SUnCNN, SRE=9.38 dB, aRMSE=8.13)

task_138.name: unmixing_SUnSAL_DC1
task_138.title: Sparse Unmixing (SUnSAL)
task_138.description: This code performs semi-supervised sparse hyperspectral unmixing on synthetic DC1 data using SUnSAL (Sparse Unmixing via variable Splitting and Augmented Lagrangian). The forward model is a linear mixing model Y = D·x where D is a known spectral dictionary (L×M) and x is a sparse abundance matrix (M×N). The inversion uses an ADMM framework that decomposes the problem into: (1) a least-squares subproblem solved via precomputed SVD of D^T·D, (2) an L1 soft-thresholding step for sparsity, and optionally (3) non-negativity projection and (4) sum-to-one constraints. The algorithm includes adaptive penalty parameter updates (mu doubling/halving) based on primal-dual residual ratios, enabling automatic convergence tuning.

metrics.eval_type: Semi-supervised sparse unmixing (SUnSAL, SRE=8.72 dB, aRMSE=8.77)
